legal_header_intro_p1: Projekt City is a private, independent project provided to you completely free of charge as part of itsmarian.dev. By downloading, you receive a compressed ZIP file which you can unzip and load directly into Minecraft - at your own risk.
legal_header_intro_p2: Please note that Projekt City is managed by itsmarian.dev. The legally required Imprint (Legal Notice) and central contact information can be found at ${imprint_link}.
legal_header_intro_p3: By continuing, you confirm that you have read and agree to our ${at}, ${eula_link}, ${privacy_link}, ${terms_link}, and ${world_link}. If you do not agree, please leave our website and refrain from using our services.
s0_p1: Web accessibility is a crucial aspect of online platform design and development that is often underestimated. It refers to how easy it is for people with different abilities and limitations to access, understand and use a website. At its core, it is about ensuring that all users, regardless of their individual needs and requirements, have the same opportunity to access and navigate digital content.
s0_p2: Web accessibility not only affects people with disabilities, but also older people, people with temporary disabilities (such as injuries) and users with slower internet connections or outdated technological devices. By removing barriers and improving accessibility, we are creating a more inclusive online environment that has the potential to enrich and improve the lives of millions of people.
s0_p3: In this article, we'll take a closer look at what web accessibility means, why it's important and how it can be achieved to ensure an inclusive and equal online experience for all. This is the accessibility statement from Projekt City Ltd.!
s1_p: Projekt City Ltd. takes the following measures to ensure accessibility of Projekt City:
s1_li1: Assign clear accessibility goals and responsibilities
s1_li2_prefix: A simple and easy to understand version of our main page:
s1_li3: Include accessibility throughout our internal policies
s1_li4: Using a uniform system for all web pages and simple colors, font families and font sizes
s2_p: The ${wcag_link} defines requirements for designers and developers to improve accessibility for people with disabilities. It defines three levels of conformance: Level A, Level AA, and Level AAA. Projekt City is partially conformant with WCAG 2.1 level AA. Partially conformant means that some parts of the content do not fully conform to the accessibility standard.
s3_p: We welcome your feedback on the accessibility of Projekt City. Please let us know if you encounter accessibility barriers on Projekt City:
s4_title: #4 Compatibility with browsers and assistive technology
s4_p: Projekt City is designed to be compatible with the following assistive technologies:
s4_li1: Browser Brave on operating System Android 10 - Android 15 and above, HyperOS, iOS 15 - iOS 18 and above, Meta Horizon OS v38 - v74 and above, macOS 10 (Ventura) - macOS 26 (Tahoe) and above, Windows 10 & Windows 11 and above
s4_li2: Browser Chrome on operating System Android 10 - Android 15 and above, HyperOS, iOS 15 - iOS 18 and above, Meta Horizon OS v38 - v74 and above, macOS 10 (Ventura) - macOS 26 (Tahoe) and above, Windows 10 & Windows 11 and above
s4_li3: Browser DuckDuckGo on operating System Android 10 - Android 15 and above, HyperOS, iOS 15 - iOS 18 and above, Meta Horizon OS v38 - v74 and above, macOS 10 (Ventura) - macOS 26 (Tahoe) and above, Windows 10 & Windows 11 and above
s4_li4: Browser Ecosia on operating System Android 10 - Android 15 and above, HyperOS, iOS 15 - iOS 18 and above, Meta Horizon OS v38 - v74 and above, macOS 10 (Ventura) - macOS 26 (Tahoe) and above, Windows 10 & Windows 11 and above
s4_li5: Browser Firefox on operating System Android 10 - Android 15 and above, HyperOS, iOS 15 - iOS 18 and above, Meta Horizon OS v38 - v74 and above, macOS 10 (Ventura) - macOS 26 (Tahoe) and above, Windows 10 & Windows 11 and above
s4_li6: Browser Microsoft Edge on operating System Android 10 - Android 15 and above, HyperOS, iOS 15 - iOS 18 and above, Meta Horizon OS v38 - v74 and above, macOS 10 (Ventura) - macOS 26 (Tahoe) and above, Windows 10 & Windows 11 and above
s4_li7: Browser Opera on operating System Android 10 - Android 15 and above, HyperOS, iOS 15 - iOS 18 and above, Meta Horizon OS v38 - v74 and above, macOS 10 (Ventura) - macOS 26 (Tahoe) and above, Windows 10 & Windows 11 and above
s4_li8: Browser Opera GX on operating System Android 10 - Android 15 and above, HyperOS, iOS 15 - iOS 18 and above, Meta Horizon OS v38 - v74 and above, macOS 10 (Ventura) - macOS 26 (Tahoe) and above, Windows 10 & Windows 11 and above
s4_li9: Browser Safari on operating System macOS 10 (Ventura) - macOS 26 (Tahoe), iOS 15 - iOS 18 and above
s4_li10: Browser TOR on operating System Android 10 - Android 15 and above, HyperOS, iOS 15 - iOS 18 and above, Meta Horizon OS v38 - v74 and above, macOS 10 (Ventura) - macOS 26 (Tahoe) and above, Windows 10 & Windows 11 and above
s4_note: It is important to mention that this information was only tested on special devices. However, it is possible that - depending on the device and its software version and type - the page may not work on an operating system listed here. Nevertheless, Projekt City always tries to adapt to this software and its devices.
s5_p1: Accessibility of Projekt City relies on the following technologies to work with the particular combination of web browser and any assistive technologies or plugins installed on your computer:
s5_p2: These technologies are relied upon for conformance with the accessibility standards used.
s6_p: This statement was created on [April 1, 2024] and was last updated on [March 1, 2026]
